name: Conlang Rosetta
capability_claim: Reliably induce a small, novel morphological+syntactic system from ~7 interlinear-glossed example sentences and produce a single exact-string translation that correctly applies word order, suffix ordering, adjective placement, tense, and negation.
task_format: One-shot translation: solver outputs predictions.jsonl with {id, answer} for each of 30 items.
scoring: Exact normalized-string match (lowercase, single-spaced, punctuation stripped). 30 items, 1 point each.
closest_existing_benchmarks: LINGOLY / Linguini benchmarks for LLM linguistic reasoning
novelty_argument: Linguistics-Olympiad-style items have been used in research benchmarks, but those use a small fixed set of real or human-authored languages and known answer keys that can leak. Conlang Rosetta procedurally generates fresh fictional languages per seed with no real-world precedent, eliminating training-data contamination. Unlike clean search/simulation benchmarks (e.g. the prior 005/004 candidates), there is no single obvious algorithm: the solver must perform abductive grammatical inference, identify suffix-ordering interactions, and apply chained rules to a held-out gloss. Each item exposes a different randomly-sampled grammar so a single brittle script cannot solve all 30.
